input.background: A questionnaire measures an index of risk-taking behavior in respondents. Scores are standardized so that 100 represents the population average. The questionnaire is applied to a sample of teenage boy and girls.
input.question: Explore group differences with side-by-side boxplots.

output.method: 离散型-连续型变量关系
output.answer: The girls have lower scores on average and less variability. There is one outlier in the girls.

risk_score.description: Standardized index score of risk-taking behavior for each respondent (mean of population = 100).

gender.description: Gender group of each teenage respondent, used to compare risk-taking scores.

answer.value: Use gender as the grouping variable and risk_score as the outcome. Create side-by-side boxplots of risk_score by gender (one box for boys, one for girls) to visually compare the central tendency, spread, and potential outliers of risk-taking behavior between the two groups.
answer.description: Procedure for exploring group differences using side-by-side boxplots.

meta_info.caption: Risk-taking behavior in boys and girls.
